sectionName: Chemical & Physical Foundations of Biological Systems

questions.passage: A physiology lab uses a simple circuit to model current flow through tissue. A 12 V source is connected to two parallel resistors, R1 = 6 Ω and R2 = 3 Ω. Students measure branch currents and total current. The R1 branch carries 2 A, the R2 branch carries 4 A, and the source current is 6 A. The lab then replaces the parallel pair with a single equivalent resistor and compares power dissipated by each branch.
questions.stem: What is the equivalent resistance of the parallel combination?

explanations.C: Correct. For parallel resistors, 1/Req = 1/6 + 1/3 = 1/2, so Req = 2 Ω. This also matches V/I = 12 V/6 A = 2 Ω.
explanations.A: 9 Ω is the series sum, not the parallel equivalent.
explanations.B: 4.5 Ω is not obtained from the correct reciprocal sum.
explanations.D: 18 Ω incorrectly multiplies the resistances without applying the parallel formula.

questions.passageImageCaption: Figure 1. Measured branch currents for two parallel resistors connected to a 12 V source.

explanations.A: Correct. In parallel, both branches have 12 V. Power is V²/R, so the lower resistance branch dissipates more power: 144/3 = 48 W versus 144/6 = 24 W.
explanations.B: The 6 Ω branch has half the current and lower power.
explanations.C: Equal voltage does not mean equal power when resistance differs.
explanations.D: Resistors dissipate electrical energy as thermal energy.

questions.stem: What is the total power supplied by the battery?

explanations.D: Correct. Total power is P = IV = 6 A × 12 V = 72 W. It also equals 24 W + 48 W from the branches.
explanations.A: 12 W uses voltage alone without current.
explanations.B: 6 W uses current alone without voltage.
explanations.C: 24 W is only the power in the 6 Ω branch.

questions.stem: If another resistor were added in parallel, what would happen to total resistance?

choices.D: It would equal the largest branch resistance

explanations.B: Correct. Adding a parallel path increases total conductance, so equivalent resistance decreases. This is why total current rises when more parallel branches are added.
explanations.A: Parallel branches reduce equivalent resistance rather than increasing it.
explanations.C: A new conducting branch changes the equivalent resistance.
explanations.D: The equivalent resistance of parallel elements is less than the smallest branch resistance.

questions.stem: Which feature of parallel circuits is most relevant to modeling multiple ion channels in a membrane?

choices.C: Each open channel provides an additional conductance pathway
choices.A: Each channel must carry the same current
choices.D: The voltage across parallel channels must be different

explanations.C: Correct. Open ion channels act like conductance pathways in parallel across a membrane. More open channels increase total conductance and lower resistance.
explanations.A: Parallel branches have the same voltage, but branch currents can differ depending on conductance.
explanations.B: Adding open channels lowers, not raises, resistance.
explanations.D: Parallel elements share the same potential difference.

questions.passage: A membrane patch is modeled as a resistor and capacitor. After a brief depolarizing pulse, membrane voltage decays exponentially toward baseline. In one condition, R = 50 MΩ and C = 100 pF, giving a time constant τ = RC = 5 ms. Voltage is recorded every 5 ms after the pulse: 40 mV, 15 mV, 5.5 mV, and 2.0 mV above baseline. A drug that increases membrane resistance is predicted to slow the decay.
questions.stem: What is the time constant of the membrane model?

explanations.D: Correct. The passage gives R = 50 MΩ and C = 100 pF. RC = 50×10⁶ × 100×10⁻¹² = 5×10⁻³ s = 5 ms.
explanations.A: 0.5 ms is too small by a factor of 10.
explanations.B: 50 ms is too large by a factor of 10.
explanations.C: 500 ms is two orders of magnitude too large.

questions.passageImageCaption: Figure 2. Exponential decay of membrane voltage in an RC membrane model with τ ≈ 5 ms.

questions.stem: After one time constant, the voltage should fall to about what fraction of its initial value?

explanations.A: Correct. For exponential discharge, V = V0e⁻¹ after one time constant, which is about 0.37V0. The graph shows 40 mV falling to roughly 15 mV after 5 ms, consistent with this.
explanations.B: 50% corresponds to a half-life, not one RC time constant.
explanations.C: 63% is the fraction lost during one time constant, not the fraction remaining.
explanations.D: A capacitor does not remain at its initial voltage during discharge.

questions.stem: What would increasing membrane resistance do to the voltage decay?

choices.B: Make it slower by increasing τ
choices.A: Make it faster by decreasing τ

explanations.B: Correct. Because τ = RC, increasing resistance increases the time constant. A larger time constant means voltage decays more slowly.
explanations.A: Increasing R increases, not decreases, τ.
explanations.C: τ depends directly on R, so it changes.
explanations.D: Changing resistance does not remove the membrane capacitance.

questions.stem: What does the capacitor represent in a membrane model?

choices.C: Charge separation across the lipid bilayer
choices.A: Ion channels carrying current through pores

explanations.C: Correct. A membrane capacitance arises because the lipid bilayer separates charge across a thin insulating layer. That is analogous to a capacitor’s two conducting plates separated by a dielectric.
explanations.A: Ion channels are better modeled as conductances/resistors.
explanations.B: ATP production is not the electrical capacitance being modeled.
explanations.D: Cytoskeleton may affect cell shape, but it is not the main capacitor analogy.

questions.stem: If capacitance doubled while resistance stayed fixed, τ would do what?

explanations.A: Correct. The time constant is τ = RC. Doubling C while holding R fixed doubles τ, slowing the voltage decay.
explanations.B: Halving would occur if capacitance were halved.
explanations.C: τ depends on capacitance, so it would not stay fixed.
explanations.D: Resistance and capacitance are positive, so τ is positive.
